terms.back: A temporary endeavor undertaken to create a unique product, service, or result.
Ongoing activities that sustain products, services, and organizational functions.
Having a defined beginning and end; it does not necessarily mean short duration.
The excess of financial and nonfinancial benefits over the investment used to achieve goals.
A consensus that the project delivered value worth the effort and expense.
The integrated set of portfolios, programs, projects, products, and operations aligned with strategy.
Related projects managed in a coordinated manner to obtain benefits not available from managing them separately.
Programs, projects, and operations managed as a group to achieve strategic objectives and maximize value.
The framework, functions, and processes that guide authority, decisions, oversight, and value delivery.
An individual, group, or organization that can affect, be affected by, or perceive itself to be affected by a project.
Deliberately adapting the approach, governance, processes, and practices to fit the context.
The individuals performing project work to achieve the project's objectives.
